summary:
• U.S. potential blockade of the Hormuz Strait poses a critical supply-route risk to Western Hemisphere markets, with ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East threatening one of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoints.

• China's collapsing LNG imports and reduced fuel consumption are reshaping global energy trade flows, potentially redirecting Middle Eastern and Atlantic Basin supply away from traditional Asian markets toward the Americas.

• Declining crude inventories amid strong product draws indicate tightening supply fundamentals in the Western Hemisphere, exacerbated by ongoing geopolitical disruptions and infrastructure disputes affecting North American crude transport capacity.

articles.title: China’s LNG Imports Collapse to Six-Year Low as Prices Surge
articles.description: China’s imports of liquefied natural gas this month are expected to be the lowest since 2018, based on data from Kpler, Bloomberg has reported, citing soaring prices. articles.title: BP reports 453% surge in net profit to $3.8bn in Q1 2026
articles.description: BP reported a net profit attributable to its shareholders of $3.8bn for the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, an increase of 453.1% from $687m in the same period of 2025.

articles.title: The Oil Supply Shock Will Scar the World for Years
